title: Fedora 34 podman rootless container accessing host
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
I am trying to access host machine from podman container with default container network settings as in Fedora 34. I can't.
I see that default gateway in container is 10.0.2.2 but then trying to access it I see:

```
$ podman run --name porxy --rm --add-host=host.docker.internal:10.0.2.2 -p 3008:3008 -ti --entrypoint /bin/bash quay.io/openshift/origin-cli:4.6
[root@fa8d95cd7b12 /]# ip route list
default via 10.0.2.2 dev tap0 
10.0.2.0/24 dev tap0 proto kernel scope link src 10.0.2.100 
[root@fa8d95cd7b12 /]# curl 10.0.2.2:3000
curl: (7) Failed to connect to 10.0.2.2 port 3000: Network is unreachable
```

That also begs another question. I [docs](https://podman.io/getting-started/network.html#container---container-communication) I see `Host_IP_Address`. Can it be documented what is `Host_IP_Address`?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `$ podman run --name porxy --rm --add-host=host.docker.internal:10.0.2.2 -p 3008:3008 -ti --entrypoint /bin/bash quay.io/openshift/origin-cli:4.6`

2. `# curl 10.0.2.2:3000`

**Describe the results you received:**
```
curl: (7) Failed to connect to 10.0.2.2 port 3000: Network is unreachable
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I would expect either a successful response or at least some more reasonable error than `Network is unreachable`. Maybe connection refused or whatever,  just at least reach the host.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.1.2
API Version:  3.1.2
Go Version:   go1.16
Built:        Thu Apr 22 16:11:28 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
physical

comments.body:
You need --net slirp4netns:allow_host_loopback=true .
This is set to false by default for security reason.

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v3.1.2/docs/source/markdown/podman-pod-create.1.md

@AkihiroSuda , this makes sense, thank you. How about updating the getting started guide with this information? Possible to reopen and assign to somebody with a good idea how to do?

https://podman.io/getting-started/network.html#container---container-communication
Can I help with the documentation if possible? any pointers would help!
@afro-coder Look at the podman.io git repo and add docs to suggested lines above.
